race.name: Minocqua Northwoods Loppet
race.tagline: Through the lake-dotted Northwoods of central Wisconsin — 25 kilometers of frozen beauty in America's island city.

climate.description: Late January in the Northwoods brings reliable cold and snow cover. The lake-rich terrain moderates temperatures slightly compared to open prairie. Temperatures -20°C to -8°C.
challenges: Extreme cold possible in late January
Flat terrain means wind exposure along lake shores
Declining snow reliability in milder recent winters

course.primary: A 25km race through the Northwoods lake country around Minocqua. The course winds through pine and hardwood forest, crossing frozen lake shores and connecting trail systems in Wisconsin's famous lake district. Mostly flat to gently rolling terrain.
course.surface: Machine-groomed Nordic trails through Northwoods forest
course.grooming: Good quality grooming by local club
features: Lake-dotted Northwoods landscape — trails pass multiple frozen lakes
Minocqua is known as the 'Island City' for its lake-surrounded location
Classic Northwoods pine and hardwood forest setting
Part of Wisconsin's strong citizen Nordic racing tradition
Accessible for families with shorter distance option

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: A solid community loppet in Wisconsin's beautiful lake country. Flat terrain, small field, and limited competitive depth keep scores modest. The Northwoods lake landscape is the defining feature.

history.summary: Minocqua, known as the 'Island City' for its location amid northern Wisconsin's thousands of lakes, has a strong Nordic skiing tradition. The loppet celebrates the Northwoods winter and draws from the region's community of lake-country residents and winter visitors.
notable_facts: Minocqua is called the 'Island City' — surrounded by lakes
The Wisconsin Northwoods have over 15,000 lakes
Nordic skiing has been popular in the area since Scandinavian settlers arrived
The race passes near several frozen lakes along the course
